topic: Missing arguments — cross-cutting insights from Phase 1 that aren't captured anywhere
change_summary: Operator-commissioned Phase 2 rerun (commission 2026-05-28T05:55Z) after ~6 weeks of substantive content changes since original April 6 holistic pass. Pipeline state at commission: open_issues=0, queue_depth=0, drought_day=41. Today's actual queue state at run time: 4 queue items all already reviewed; no human notes; Step 0c2 audit not triggered (no critical/strategic changes this week); priority inversion applied per Step 0d override clause. Scan compared current sections.json + wins.json against April-6 HOL-MISSING-flagged missing arguments (10 of them) and looked for new missing arguments arising from material integrated since.

operator_commission_reference.rationale_acknowledged: Phase 2 rerun: re-evaluate cross-WIN/cross-section/prose-level integrity after content changes. Find structural issues no per-WIN/per-SEC review can catch.

our_argument_summary: The April 6 HOL-MISSING pass flagged 10 cross-cutting arguments missing from the review (quadruple-failure, axial-symmetry, batch-registration, duplicate-counting, derives-from-dome devastation, monitor-doesn't-monitor, kernels-of-truth synthesis, falsifiability asymmetry, eclipse pre-commitment, open-problems concession). ~6 weeks later: 7 of 10 have landed substantively in prose (axial-symmetry: 3 hits; batch-registration: 6 hits; eclipse-pre-commitment: 48 hits; open-problems: 37 hits; kernels-of-truth: 16 hits; derives-from-dome devastation: 5 hits with a 'Derives-from-Dome Problem' subsection in Part 2b containing the 'Zero of 72' four-criteria framing; cosmological-incoherence: gestured to via Tier 3 framing). 3 of 10 are still substantively missing (falsifiability symmetry / 'what would change OUR verdict'; quadruple-failure as a top-of-document headline statistic; the post-April reactive-prose-vs-structural-fix meta-pattern). The 'Derives-from-Dome Problem' subsection that DID land now has a stale enumeration drift (lists 4 exceptions when only 1 WIN currently has derives_from_dome=true). Three NEW cross-cutting gaps have emerged from material landed since April 6.

relevant_files: data/sections.json (13 section keys including part1, part2, part2b, part8b)
dome_code_analysis.calculation_pipeline: Holistic scan: enumerated 13 section HTML blobs (~677K chars total) and ran 30+ keyword/regex patterns testing for prior-cycle missing arguments and new missing-argument candidates. Tallied wins.json code_analysis tags directly to verify whether headline statistics in prose still match underlying data.
dome_code_analysis.hardcoded_vs_computed: Mixed. Part 2b uses {{CA_REVIEWED}}, {{CA_DOME}}, {{TOTAL_WINS}} placeholders for the derives-from-dome stat (computed). But the enumeration of FOUR exceptions (WIN-020/044/057/058) is hardcoded prose and now drifts — only WIN-044 still has derives_from_dome=true. EXP-441/442 (queue items 418/419) just landed templates for the §2b.1 stratification and contrast paragraphs; the §2b.4 'Derives-from-Dome Problem' subsection has not yet received the same treatment.
dome_code_analysis.code_discrepancies: Part 2b §2b.4 says '{{CA_DOME}} exceptions deserve individual scrutiny' then enumerates four WINs. {{CA_DOME}} currently renders as '1' (only WIN-044). The four-exception list (020/044/057/058) is a relic from when those tags were true. The prose argument that follows ('Zero of 72 meet all four criteria') is still strong — but the lead-in is now a templated-vs-hardcoded count mismatch of exactly the kind we criticize the dome for in Part 2b §2b.1.

code_analysis_tags.monitoring_evidence: Holistic review, no per-WIN automation; N/A by convention but recorded for schema compliance.
code_analysis_tags.relabels_standard_evidence: Holistic review of cross-cutting argument coverage; no claim being relabeled.
code_analysis_tags.post_hoc_evidence: Forward-looking gap analysis of our own document.

holes_found.description: DERIVES-FROM-DOME enumeration drift: Part 2b §2b.4 'The Derives-from-Dome Problem' uses {{CA_DOME}} (currently=1) in two sentences ('only {{CA_DOME}} actually derives...', 'The {{CA_DOME}} exceptions deserve individual scrutiny') and then enumerates FOUR exceptions (WIN-020/044/057/058). Only WIN-044 currently has derives_from_dome=true in wins.json; the other three were re-tagged to false during the curmudgeon code_analysis revalidation cycles. The reader sees 'only 1' followed by four entries — visible templated-count vs. hardcoded-list contradiction. This is structurally identical to the §2b.1 stratification bug (ISS-2273/EXP-441) which is currently mid-integration. We criticize the dome for exactly this failure mode ('static counts that no longer match the underlying registry') in Part 2b §2b.1's own rebuke. Same bug, our prose.
holes_found.recommendation: Apply the EXP-441/442 templating treatment to §2b.4. Convert the four-WIN enumeration into a build-time-generated list driven by code_analysis.derives_from_dome=true. Either: (a) render only WIN-044 as the single exception and move the 020/057/058 discussion to a 'previously claimed as derivations but reclassified after audit' supplementary list (with a one-line note about the reclassification record); or (b) keep the four-WIN forensic narrative but rewrite the lead-in to 'Four WINs were initially tagged as derives_from_dome=true; after detailed scrutiny, three were reclassified to false. The single remaining exception is WIN-044, and even that is suspended pending August 2026 eclipse data.' Both options eliminate the count-vs-list drift. Suggest creating an issue routed to decider for analyst Mode 2/3 expansion.

holes_found.description: FALSIFIABILITY SYMMETRY — 'What would change OUR verdict' is still missing as a cross-cutting promise. Original April 6 HOL-MISSING flagged this as Argument #8. EXP-451 (HOL-STRESSTEST c2) authored 2026-05-30 specifically addresses the falsifiability-module tautology critique but is not yet integrated, and even its scope is the dome-side falsifiability circularity defense, not the symmetric question. The Overview falsifiability tile says '0 Survive Scrutiny' / 'What would change this?' — but the answer ('our criteria are stated in the Evaluation Guide') is tab-distance away. Phrase-search for 'change our verdict' / 'change our minds' / 'falsify OUR review' across all 13 sections returns 2 hits, both inside §4.2.6 about one specific eclipse claim. Nowhere in Part 1 (Overview), Part 7 (Falsification Tests), or Part 9 (Conclusions) does the review state inline what a single observation would force us to revise — even though every honest scientific critique should make that promise visible. ISS-2284 (open) addresses the related dome-side circularity charge but the symmetric self-falsifiability commitment is still uncovered.
holes_found.recommendation: Add a 2–4 sentence inline 'What would change OUR verdict' panel directly under the '0 Survive Scrutiny' tile in Part 1, listing 3–4 named conditions (e.g., 'a single dome-derived prediction confirmed by independent measurement that standard physics cannot reproduce', 'an August 2026 eclipse anomaly matching dome geometry's umbra-locked timing', 'identification of a four-criteria-passing WIN our audit missed', 'a successful structural OTS canary trip showing the dome separated prediction-publication from observation-receipt'). Cross-link from Part 7 and Part 9. This is the falsifiability-symmetry argument the April 6 HOL-MISSING already requested; it has now been deferred for ~6 weeks while EXP-451's dome-side hardening took precedence.

holes_found.description: REACTIVE-PROSE-VS-STRUCTURAL-FIX pattern is NEW and uncaptured. EXP-435 (2026-05-28, dome-review-response-coverage-audit) explicitly identified this as 'the canonical reactive-prose-not-structural-fix pattern' in the dome's response page: prose edits ('scoping clarified') without corresponding OTS / prediction-isolation / methodology fixes. This is a methodologically devastating cross-cutting observation: when the dome author updates verbiage in response to critique while leaving the load-bearing structures (the OTS canary, the answer-sheet timestamping, the unfalsifiable 100% denominator) untouched, the dome's claim to be a scientific framework is undercut by its own pattern of repair. Per CLAUDE.md author-contact addendum (2026-04-27), the author IS reading our review and reactively patching. Yet zero prose hits for 'reactive prose', 'reactive update', 'structural fix' (only 2 hits, neither in this context), or any synthesis paragraph distinguishing 'narrative responsiveness' from 'methodological correction'. The Timestamp Error tab exists and the canary is unclipped — but no section synthesizes 'updates to prose without updates to OTS/prediction-isolation = apologetics mode, not science mode'.
holes_found.recommendation: Write a new ~200-word cross-cutting paragraph for either Timestamp Error tab (most natural home — it already covers the OTS error) or Part 8b (Fair-Auditor List). Suggested framing: 'The dome model has updated its prose multiple times in response to specific critiques cataloged here (versions V50.6 → V51.0 → V51.1, the review-response.html page, the 'scoping clarified' addition). Updates we have observed are uniformly prose-level: re-wordings, qualifier additions, framing softenings. The structural commitments — independent OTS timestamping of predictions before observations arrive, a falsifiable accuracy denominator that includes silent failures, a derivation path from dome parameters to numerical claims — have not been corrected. This pattern of narrative responsiveness without methodological repair is itself a finding: a scientific framework would respond to a list of self-contradictions by changing the parameters or the predictions; a rhetorical framework responds by adjusting the prose. The OTS canary remains untripped after [N] reactive prose updates.' Route to analyst Mode 2 for proper sourcing.

holes_found.description: QUADRUPLE-FAILURE HEADLINE STAT is still missing at the top of the review. Recomputed today: 48 of 72 WINs (67%) simultaneously fail all four code_analysis criteria (monitoring=hardcoded-or-none + relabels_standard + post_hoc + NOT derives_from_dome). Original April 6 HOL-MISSING flagged this as 'Missing Cross-Cutting Argument #1' with stat 34/64 = 53%. The stat has gotten worse in 6 weeks. Part 2b 'Zero of {{TOTAL_WINS}} meet all four criteria' carries the strict 'all four' version. But the 'quadruple structural failure' framing — that 67% of WINs simultaneously exhibit all four signs of not-actually-scientific-prediction — is not stated anywhere as a single number. The Overview's '0 Survive Scrutiny' tile carries the strictest cut; the 67% intermediate cut is invisible. A reader who finds 'Zero' improbable will more easily accept '48 of 72 share all four red flags' as a stepping stone.
holes_found.recommendation: Add a single sentence to Part 2b §2b.1 (after the templating EXP-441/442 land) or to the Overview falsifiability module: 'Of 72 reviewed WINs, 48 (67%) simultaneously satisfy all four structural-failure criteria: hardcoded or no monitoring + relabels standard physics + adopts a published value post-hoc + no derivation from dome geometry. The remaining 24 WINs each fail two or three of the four criteria; the strictest test — all four criteria simultaneously passed (a dome-derived, prospective, non-trivial, automation-monitored prediction) — is passed by zero.' Quantitative_verification confirmed (48/72 computed from current wins.json).

holes_found.description: LIVING-DOCUMENT acknowledgment is still missing, despite ISS-2286 (open) flagging this as one of three moderate items in batch. Zero prose hits for 'living document', 'continually updated', 'under continuous'. The review is, in fact, a living document — 539 EXPs landed since project start, holistic checks rerunning, change-driven reviews etc. — but readers see a static-feeling document and a defender can charge 'this review is a snapshot of an obsolete dome version' without our text preempting that. A 2-sentence Overview disclosure ('This review is under continuous revision. Verdicts are dated; the dome model's updates are tracked; changes are logged at [link]') closes the gap.
holes_found.recommendation: Tie into ISS-2286 batch resolution. Add a 'Living Review' line either in the existing 'How This Review Is Organized' Overview block or as a sibling disclosure near the 'Last updated' footer. Sentence template provided. The version archive (Part 1b: V50.6 → V51.0 analysis) exists — use it as the cross-reference target.

holes_found.description: SYMMETRIC-STANDARDS / 'we hold ourselves to the same criteria' cross-cutting promise is still missing as Overview content. Zero hits for 'apply the same criteria to us', 'hold ourselves', 'mirror our own criteria'. Part 8b ('Fair-Auditor List — What Changes If We Follow It') is the closest existing surface and is excellent — but the Overview never makes the symmetric promise. EXP-435 found the dome's own response page implicitly accusing us of asymmetric application (e.g. preserving prospective/retrospective distinctions). Without an inline Overview statement, that charge has rhetorical room. A defender can land a 'they apply different rules to themselves than to me' attack before the reader ever reaches Part 8b.
holes_found.recommendation: One Overview sentence — proposed: 'The criteria applied to dome predictions in this review are the same criteria we apply to our own counterclaims: derivation from stated principles, prospective commitment, non-trivial differentiation from alternatives, independent verification. Where we fall short, see Part 8b; the four conditions that would change our overall verdict are listed in [Falsifiability Module].' Cross-link to Part 8b and to the new 'What would change OUR verdict' panel proposed in Hole #2.

holes_found.description: SC-list undersell remains (ISS-2276 open since recent HOL-NARRATIVE c2): 20 WINs are tagged Self-Contradicted but Part 2 features only 9 by name. The other 11 are scattered or undiscussed. This is connected to the missing-arguments theme because the cross-cutting argument 'Self-Contradiction is the dome's largest verdict bucket' loses force when most of the 20 are invisible. EXP-450 (HOL-HIERARCHY c2 restructure) introduces 3 new SC subsections (κ-divide-by-zero, 1,660:1, …); after that integration the SC-visibility gap will narrow. Flagging here so the HOL-MISSING review history reflects that the cross-cutting argument is structurally weaker than its raw count suggests.
holes_found.recommendation: Land EXP-450 first (already queued); then revisit whether all 20 SC WINs have an in-prose anchor either in Part 2, Part 2b, or one of the SC-grouped subsections. If 5+ remain anchorless, consider a Part 2 'Other Self-Contradictions' index table.

stronger_arguments.description: PROMOTE 'Zero of 72 WINs meet all four criteria' from Part 2b §2b.4 (currently at ~27% through the document) to the Overview falsifiability module. Replace the standalone '0 Survive Scrutiny' tile's sub-label ('None produces a dome-derived prediction…') with the explicit four-criteria framing: 'Zero of 72 reviewed predictions are simultaneously (a) derived from dome geometry, (b) stated before observation, (c) non-trivial vs. standard physics, and (d) independently confirmed.' Currently this is the strongest single sentence in the review and it appears mid-Part-2b. Most readers never see it.
sources: current data/wins.json with derives_from_dome=true count=1 (WIN-044 only)

stronger_arguments.description: PROMOTE the 'Reactive prose, untripped canary' synthesis (currently in EXP-435 internal-only memo) into the Timestamp Error tab as its own sub-section. The Timestamp Error tab already explains the OTS canary; adding 'and here's what we've observed since the canary was set' makes the canary a working instrument, not a passive trap. With each cycle that adds another reactive-prose update without an OTS-protected prediction-isolation fix, this section gets stronger.

TIE the falsifiability symmetry argument (missing argument #2 above) to the OTS canary status (missing argument #3 above). Single Overview block: 'What would change our verdict' → list 4 conditions, one of which is 'a successful OTS canary trip (independent prediction-document anchoring before observations arrive)'. This binds the falsifiability-symmetry promise to a specific, mechanically-observable signal we are already monitoring. Defender cannot claim our falsifiability conditions are vague — one of them is a binary cron-checkable file existence test.
sources: monitor/poller change tracking on dome ts/ directory

stronger_arguments.description: Cross-tabulate Self-Contradicted verdict (20) × derives_from_dome (1 of 1 SC WINs has derives_from_dome=true: WIN-044). Computed today: 19 SC WINs have derives_from_dome=false, 1 has true. Useful cross-cutting observation: the dome's only self-contradiction-passing geometric derivation (WIN-044 FSF) is also its only positive derives_from_dome tag. That tag was the precondition for the self-contradiction to even register — without derivation, there is no internal contradiction, only an unsupported assertion. Worth a 1-sentence aside in Part 2b §2b.4: 'WIN-044 is simultaneously the only derives_from_dome=true tag and a Self-Contradicted verdict; the latter is only possible because of the former.'
sources: wins.json verdict × code_analysis cross-tabulation, computed in this review

kernel_of_truth.description: The review has substantially improved on the April 6 HOL-MISSING baseline. 7 of the 10 originally-missing cross-cutting arguments have landed in prose (axisymmetric (sections.json:part2), batch-registration (sections.json:part2b), eclipse-pre-commitment (sections.json:part4 §4.2.6), open-problems-concession (sections.json:part4, part6, part8b), kernels-of-truth-synthesis (sections.json:part3, part6), eclipse / cosmological gestures (sections.json:part1 tier framing)). The 'Derives-from-Dome Problem' subsection in Part 2b (sections.json:part2b 'Zero of {{TOTAL_WINS}} WINs meet all four criteria') is the most consequential addition and stands as the review's single strongest synthetic sentence. The recent c2 holistic cycle (HOLISTIC-HOL-COMPLETENESS.c3 at monitor/curmudgeon/reviews/HOLISTIC-HOL-COMPLETENESS.c3.json; HOLISTIC-HOL-NARRATIVE.c2; HOLISTIC-HOL-STRESSTEST.c2) is delivering exactly the kind of substantive structural improvements the operator commission hoped for.
kernel_of_truth.why_it_doesnt_save_claim: Substantial progress, but the specific deliverable of HOL-MISSING — 'cross-cutting arguments not captured anywhere' — has not reached zero. Three originally-missing arguments are still substantively absent (falsifiability symmetry, quadruple-failure as headline stat, the now-newly-relevant reactive-prose-pattern). One originally-flagged argument (derives-from-dome) DID land but with a count-vs-list drift bug (sections.json:part2b §2b.4 enumerates four exceptions where {{CA_DOME}}=1). The structural pattern HOL-MISSING is designed to catch — substantive insights stranded in EXPs or in analyst memos without making it into reader-visible prose — is still active: EXP-435's reactive-prose synthesis is the most consequential current example. Verdict stays current_verdict_holds=false because the review still has missing arguments; recommended_action is major_rewrite (with specific landing targets per hole).

ISS-2274 (structural_major): V13 Coordinate System ordering — adjacent to Hole #2 (top-of-document promotion of strongest synthetic claim)
ISS-2276 (structural_major): 20 SC tagged but 9 named — same theme as Hole #7 minor
ISS-2284 (argumentation_major): falsifiability circularity defense — adjacent to Hole #2 (falsifiability symmetry); dome-side vs review-side companions
ISS-2286 (argumentation_moderate_batch): living-document acknowledgment — same theme as Hole #5
overlaps_with_recent_holistics: HOLISTIC-HOL-COMPLETENESS.c3 (2026-05-29): templating drift in §2b.1 stratification/contrast — same drift pattern as Hole #1 §2b.4
HOLISTIC-HOL-NARRATIVE.c2 (2026-05-28): falsifiability module reference quality — adjacent to Hole #2
HOLISTIC-HOL-STRESSTEST.c2 (2026-05-30 via EXP-451): falsifiability module tautology — adjacent to Hole #2 (dome-side, ours symmetric)
cross_reference_overlap.note: This review's findings are deliberately positioned NOT to duplicate per-WIN/per-SEC work. The 6 holes flagged are cross-cutting promotions and meta-synthesis gaps that no per-item review will surface.

claims_checked: current count of WINs with derives_from_dome=true (claimed: 1)
quantitative_verification.computations: Direct enumeration over wins.json (72 entries). derives_from_dome=true: 1 (WIN-044). Quadruple criterion: monitoring in {'hardcoded','none'} AND relabels_standard AND post_hoc AND NOT derives_from_dome → 48 matches. SC verdict tally: 20. The April-6 stat '34 of 64' is the prior-review datum; current snapshot 48/72 = 67% is verified.

deeper_analysis: The pattern across the 6 weeks since the original HOL-MISSING is encouraging: cross-cutting arguments DO eventually land in prose, often via the holistic check cycle (HOL-COMPLETENESS, HOL-NARRATIVE, HOL-HIERARCHY, HOL-STRESSTEST, HOL-TAXONOMY all running c2/c3 in the last fortnight). The system is working. The three remaining gaps share a feature: each requires placing a synthesis sentence at the TOP of the review (Overview falsifiability module or Timestamp Error tab) rather than in a deep tab. The dome-author DM contact (CLAUDE.md 2026-04-27) is changing the rhetorical environment: the dome is reading and reactively patching. Our review's Overview is the surface that matters most — both for first-time readers and for the AI-adversarial framing (HOL-STRESSTEST). Stranding our strongest synthetic claims in Part 2b §2b.4 (offset ~25%) is the dominant remaining failure mode of the document architecture. Counter-consideration: aggressive Overview promotion risks losing the 'evidence first, conclusion later' rhetorical discipline that gives the review its credibility. The Overview already carries strong claims (Zero Survive Scrutiny, Silent Failures, etc.); the question is whether adding 'What would change OUR verdict' + 'Reactive-prose pattern observed' + '48 of 72 quadruple-failure' tips the Overview from 'thesis-stated-and-evidenced' to 'thesis-front-loaded-and-defensive'. The proposed integrations (specific named conditions, OTS canary status sentence, single 48/72 stat) are calibrated to be evidentially anchored rather than rhetorically front-loaded, but this trade-off should be explicit when the EXPs land.

ISS-2284's exact framing of dome-side falsifiability circularity (read summary only)

summary_for_decider: HOL-MISSING c2 (operator-commissioned Phase 2 rerun). current_verdict_holds=false, recommended_action=major_rewrite. Of 10 originally-missing cross-cutting arguments (April 6), 7 have landed. 3 still missing PLUS 3 new missing arguments emerged: (i) §2b.4 'Derives-from-Dome Problem' templated-count-vs-hardcoded-list drift (only 1 derives_from_dome=true now, prose lists 4 — same bug as §2b.1 ISS-2273); (ii) falsifiability symmetry / 'What would change OUR verdict' inline panel missing from Overview; (iii) reactive-prose-vs-structural-fix meta-pattern (NEW from EXP-435 May 28 audit) is uncaptured anywhere in prose despite being the most consequential observation of the post-DM-contact era; plus moderates: (iv) 48/72 quadruple-failure headline stat missing, (v) living-document acknowledgment missing (ISS-2286 batch), (vi) symmetric-standards Overview promise missing. Plus minor: SC-list undersell (ISS-2276 covers; flagged here for the HOL-MISSING audit trail). Recommend routing as 3 separate EXPs (templating fix for §2b.4 is verification-class; falsifiability-symmetry + reactive-prose-pattern are deep-attack-class; 48/72 stat is verification-class once §2b §2b.1 templates land). Do NOT bundle into a single big EXP — each Overview promotion is its own scope and risks rhetorical balance shifts. Counter-consideration noted in deeper_analysis: aggressive Overview promotion of synthetic claims must preserve the evidence-first discipline; the specific landing language proposed in each Hole's recommendation is calibrated for that.
